senses.gloss: to follow, to succeed, to come after in a sequence

examples.text: قَوْلٌ مَعْرُوفٌ وَمَغْفِرَةٌ خَيْرٌ مِنْ صَدَقَةٍ يَتْبَعُهَا أَذًى
examples.english: A kind word with forgiveness is better than almsgiving followed by injury.

senses.gloss: to follow physically, to pursue, to move behind in the same path
to follow, to adhere to, abide by, or obey; to comply with

examples.text: فَمَنْ تَبِعَ هُدَايَ فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
examples.english: Whosoever follows My guidance, on them shall be no fear, nor shall they grieve.

senses.gloss: a king in ancient Yemen; the ruler of Himyar and Hadramawt

etymology.text: Uncertain. Lane claims that it is from the root ت ب ع (t b ʕ), in reference to how when one king died, his successor would follow his policies. The unusual plural pattern suggests a borrowing from Old South Arabian, but the cognate root in those languages likely has the same meaning of "follow", and is only attested in theophoric names.
